name: Tangipahoa Parish Teachers
description_short: Member-owned credit union serving educators in Tangipahoa Parish, LA since 1946, offering auto loans, personal loans, and savings accounts with competitive rates.
description_long:
Tangipahoa Parish Teachers' Credit Union (TPTCU) was established in 1946 to serve employees of the Tangipahoa Parish School System, Southeastern Louisiana University, and their family members. The credit union began with just 25 members and $256.50 in assets, and has grown substantially over nearly 72 years under the leadership of founder Jack Lanier and current Board President Thomas Bellavia (serving since 1979). Today, TPTCU operates as a member-owned, democratically controlled financial institution with approximately 3,800 members and over $30 million in assets.

TPTCU offers a range of financial products including new and used auto loans with competitive rates, personal loans for education and family needs, summer vacation financing specials (April-August), new car financing specials, and savings/share accounts. The credit union emphasizes member benefits and financial security, positioning itself as a resource for building credit, funding major life purchases, and retirement planning. Business hours are Monday-Friday 8:30 AM-4:30 PM, with online payment options available.

The credit union distinguishes itself through its community-focused mission, democratic member governance, and emphasis on the "people helping people" cooperative principle. Member testimonials highlight the personal service, respect, and long-term relationships TPTCU builds—members report 2-22 years of loyalty. The institution explicitly markets itself as treating members as individuals rather than numbers, and actively works to help members build credit scores and achieve financial milestones.

The primary limitation is that membership is restricted to employees of Tangipahoa Parish School System, Southeastern Louisiana University, and their immediate family members—making it inaccessible to the general public. As a smaller regional credit union with $30M in assets, TPTCU may have fewer services and product variations compared to larger financial institutions. The website provides limited specific information about rates, fees, loan terms, or advanced digital banking capabilities.
services: New auto loans with competitive rates and special financing promotions
Used auto loans with competitive rates
Personal loans for education and family expenses
Credit-building loan products (implied through member testimonials about credit score improvement)
pros: Established 1946 with nearly 72 years of stable operations and community presence
Membership eligibility includes employees of Tangipahoa Parish School System and Southeastern Louisiana University plus family members
Competitive new and used auto loan rates explicitly highlighted as a member benefit
Seasonal financing promotions including summer vacation loans and new car specials
Long-term member retention demonstrated by testimonials spanning 2-22+ years of membership
Personal, relationship-based service culture emphasized in member testimonials
Member-owned structure aligns institution incentives with member financial success
Over $30 million in assets providing institutional stability
cons: Membership is restricted to specific employee groups—not available to general public
Website lacks specific information about APRs, fees, loan terms, or minimum credit score requirements
Limited digital banking information; no mention of mobile app, online account opening, or advanced online services
Smaller asset base ($30M) compared to major banks and credit unions may limit product availability
Only one branch location mentioned; geographic accessibility limited to Tangipahoa Parish area
best_for: Tangipahoa Parish School System employees seeking auto financing and personal loans
Southeastern Louisiana University employees and their immediate family members
Teachers and educators looking to build credit and establish long-term financial relationships
Members seeking seasonal financing for major purchases like vehicles and family vacations
diagnosis: TPTCU is best for current and retired employees of Tangipahoa Parish School System and Southeastern Louisiana University who need competitive auto loans, personal loans, or savings products and value long-term personal relationships with their financial institution. The primary caveat is strict membership eligibility—general public consumers cannot access services, and the institution's smaller size may limit product breadth compared to larger lenders.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_description: Tangipahoa Parish Teachers — NCUA-insured credit union in AMITE, LA. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
